_meta.purpose: Catalog of worker models the orchestrator can delegate to via OpenCode. Refresh prices quarterly from https://models.dev.
_meta.notes: Model IDs use OpenRouter format `openrouter/<provider>/<model>`. Prices approximate. Use the catalog as data — never hardcode IDs in scripts.

code-cheap.rationale: code specialist, far cheaper than Sonnet, strong on refactors / boilerplate

code-quick.rationale: fast single-file edits, general coding, near-zero cost

code-frontier.rationale: when reasoning over a large change matters; reach for this only when cheaper models fail

summarize.rationale: 2M context king; great for transcripts and long docs

summarize-fast.rationale: short text summaries at near-zero cost

doc-synthesis.rationale: multi-file → briefing doc; structured output from large inputs

reasoning-cheap.rationale: thinking mode provides explicit chain-of-thought at fraction of frontier cost

draft.rationale: fast general-purpose drafting at near-zero cost

translate.rationale: Qwen excels at non-English tasks; cheap

extract.rationale: structured JSON extraction is Haiku's sweet spot

weaknesses: open model — discount self-reported benchmarks by ~8 months vs frontier
deepseek-v4-pro.use_when: reasoning tasks, multi-document synthesis, long-context analysis, ADR drafting. Best open alternative to Opus.

deepseek-v4-flash.use_when: default workhorse for delegation — drafts, summaries, code edits, routine tasks. Near-zero cost.

weaknesses: weaker on pure reasoning vs V4 Pro
kimi.use_when: transcripts, long doc analysis, bulk summarization, anything 256k+ tokens

qwen-coder.use_when: code generation, refactor, boilerplate, autocomplete-style tasks

qwen.use_when: translation, multilingual tasks, general writing where cost matters

haiku.use_when: structured extraction, classification, anything where you need predictable shape

weaknesses: expensive — use only when cheaper models fail
claude-sonnet.use_when: complex multi-file code work where qwen-coder or V4 Flash have tried and failed

gpt5-mini.use_when: second opinion, sanity-check, or when OpenAI-flavored output is preferred

gemini-flash.use_when: long-context with images/PDFs; fast bulk reads; vision tasks V4 Pro can't do
